summary: First-iteration full-stack testing for 'Who Are My Clients?' site. Backend: 12/12 pytest tests pass (health, leads CRUD+validation, contact CRUD+validation, resources list+filter). Frontend: Home (7 sections), Book (6 sections), Resources (3 cards), Tools (all 8 tools present with exact IDs), About, Contact verified. Chapter download modal submits to /api/leads successfully and shows success state. Contact form submits to /api/contact successfully. Navbar has 6 links + Buy button, mobile menu works, Amazon link opens in new tab (placeholder ASIN as expected).

minor.issue: Return 200 on create instead of REST-standard 201; not functionally broken.

integration_issues.issue: CHAPTERS_PDF_URL points to /assets/who-are-my-clients-first-3-chapters.pdf which is not uploaded (expected per problem statement — user will provide). Form submission to /api/leads succeeds; only the subsequent PDF fetch 404s.

action_items: Replace AMAZON_BOOK_URL placeholder ASIN with real Amazon link (in /app/frontend/src/lib/constants.js)
Upload the actual first-3-chapters PDF to /app/frontend/public/assets/who-are-my-clients-first-3-chapters.pdf (or update CHAPTERS_PDF_URL to hosted location)
Optional: return 201 Created on POST /api/leads and /api/contact for REST correctness
critical_code_review_comments: server.py inserts the dict with a .copy() before motor mutates it with _id — good. Response model uses extra='ignore' so even if _id leaked it would be stripped. Solid.
GET endpoints properly exclude _id with projection {'_id': 0} — good.
CORS currently set to '*' via env; fine for preview, should be tightened for production.
POST endpoints return 200 instead of 201 Created — minor REST deviation.
/api/resources returns a static in-memory SEED_RESOURCES list (by design per PRD) — consider moving to DB later if admin editing is needed.

ChapterDownloadModal triggers programmatic <a>.click() download — works, but a 404 on the placeholder PDF will be silently ignored by browser. Consider validating the PDF URL or surfacing an error message once real file is hosted.
EmailStr requires email-validator package; it's installed (tests pass) — confirmed.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of critical flows (home, book, resources, tools, about, contact, chapter modal, contact form, navbar, mobile menu, external link target=_blank)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created a few TEST_ prefixed leads and contacts via API during testing. Kept in DB (cleanup not implemented); non-production preview DB so acceptable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All flows green. If retesting after the real Amazon URL and PDF are swapped in, focus on: (1) AMAZON_BOOK_URL now points to a real product page, (2) PDF download returns 200 and browser saves file. No other changes needed.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing tests after first-run mobile-menu overlay false-positive (re-verified in clean session and passed).
